metadata.title: Five Materials That Could Unlock 5–12 GtCO₂/Year
metadata.deck: A compact, auditable record of the article’s claims, source figures, data, methods, and institutional responsibility.

summary.claim: Five material classes share a defect-mediated prediction problem. Correcting systematic model error at under-coordinated environments may improve which candidates reach synthesis.
summary.scope: Portfolio-level evidence record for five climate-relevant material targets.
limitations: Impact ranges are conditional scenarios, not deployment forecasts.
Commercial outcomes depend on scale-up, policy, and partner validation beyond this evidence record.
summary.lead: Five material classes share a defect-mediated prediction problem. Correcting systematic model error at under-coordinated environments may improve which candidates reach synthesis.

findings.label: Claim
findings.text: The portfolio’s combined climate opportunity is estimated at 5–12 GtCO₂/year, conditional on successful development and deployment.

findings.label: Mechanism
findings.text: Ranking errors arise where universal potentials soften transition states, surfaces, and defects.

findings.label: Boundary
findings.text: This pack records evidence and assumptions; it does not represent experimental validation of every candidate.

summary.verdictBody: The aggregate range is an independently sourced opportunity estimate, not a forecast. Each pathway retains technical, policy, and scale-up uncertainty.
summary.orientation: The following pages preserve representative article figures with captions, a compact data table, the methodology note, credits, and a bibliography. Figure numbering and source lines remain stable between builds.

figures.title: Five Material Classes Could Unlock 5–12 GtCO₂/Year
figures.alt: Horizontal comparison of five material classes and their estimated annual climate impact ranges.
figures.caption: Five independently sized material targets sum to an aggregate climate potential of 5–12 GtCO₂/year.

figures.title: Blind Prediction of Surface Energies Across 36 Model–Material Pairs
figures.alt: Scatter plot comparing measured and predicted surface-energy errors across 36 model-material pairs.
figures.caption: Across 36 model–material combinations, the environment error field predicts never-fitted surface energies with Pearson r = 0.906 and zero adjustable parameters.

dataTables.title: Representative targets
dataTables.caption: Representative targets carried from the article into the proof pack.

methodology.description: Claims are selected from the article’s explicit quantitative statements. Each retained chart must have a descriptive alternative text, visible caption, source line, and corresponding bibliography entry.
steps: Identify decision-relevant quantitative claims in the article.
Select figures that preserve complete axes, labels, legends, and annotations.
Copy values without reinterpretation; state transformations and exclusions.
Map every figure and table to independent bibliography entries.
methodology.unitOfAnalysis: Material class and defect-mediated performance metric
methodology.versioning: Deterministic HTML/CSS build; source URL and document ID printed on cover
methodology.limitations: Impact ranges are conditional scenarios; commercial outcomes are not guaranteed.
transformations: None; values are copied as stated.

bibliography.title: Systematic softening in universal machine learning interatomic potentials
Climate Change 2022: Mitigation of Climate Change
REFUEL: Renewable Energy to Fuels Through Utilization of Energy-Dense Liquids
